untranslatable_reason: Signal requires real-time order book monitoring, latency measurement (<1ms), IOC order execution tracking, hidden liquidity detection, and order ping sequencing. These are execution/microstructure parameters not expressible as OHLCV-based indicators. The strategy depends on exchange connectivity, order priority queuing, and order rejection/fill status — none of which can be represented in indicator-based rules.
translation_notes: Attempted to capture entry triggers (price at support/resistance + volume spike) and exit signals (order fill detection via volume proxy + multi-ping expiration via consecutive failures). However, the core logic of 'liquidity pinging' with IOC orders, tracking fill vs. expire states across 5 sequential probes, and latency arbitrage cannot be faithfully translated into a static indicator rule set. This strategy requires an event-driven execution engine with order state tracking.
